comment.body:
Always be patient, and avoid trading for only the sake of it.
Without conception, without plan, without thinking, there is no longer-term growth. 
Be patient with the growth.
Market won't let you to be millionaire overnight.

Most ppl fall for the mistake to try to trade everything like if somebody would put a gun to your head to do. But if I would ask, why you made this or that decision, you could not answer properly.
This is a mental trap. I tell you what I mean by that.
You have $800 on your account. You go trading and you lose $300
Next day you win again $500
Next day you lose $200, but you get a successful 'revenge trading' and win $400
You get overconfident and lose $700 on the next day. 
And finally on the last day you can gain $300.

Now listen...
 -300 + 500 - 200 + 400 - 700 + 300 = 0

Think about it...
and read my educational post about trading principles...

comment.body:
"This is crucial, because back in 2018 and in 2021, this high signals started to appear 1-month and even 90 pts (i.e. 2024 July gamma squeeze) before the actual top was in, but didn’t necessary appeared on the day of the top. What they made was to sell more vomma and long more theta to sustain the ranging and cool down the accumulated imbalances for the algos like mine too, so one cannot time it (and we are in a Trump-market again).
This is important to keep in mind. No free lunch…"
comment.raw_body:
"This is crucial, because back in 2018 and in 2021, this high signals started to appear 1-month and even 90 pts (i.e. 2024 July gamma squeeze) before the actual top was in, but didn’t necessary appeared on the day of the top. What they made was to sell more vomma and long more theta to sustain the ranging and cool down the accumulated imbalances for the algos like mine too, so one cannot time it (and we are in a Trump-market again).
This is important to keep in mind. No free lunch…"

comment.body:
"My present risk window is the PPI release and the Jackson Hole symposium. Reasons are explained. I want to see how the U.S. economy/policy handles the inflationary impact of tariffs. Either we get the correction here, or—if the communication is benign—the next risk window is the September FOMC, because after a rate cut the market will have to reprice itself. If there is a cut, it would be a hawkish cut.
By then the SPX could reach 6600—or even 7000. I’ll detail this in the next weekend post."

comment.body:
Vomma is a second-order derivative for an option’s value and demonstrates the convexity of vega. A positive value for vomma indicates that a percentage point increase in volatility will result in an increased option value, which is demonstrated by vega's convexity.
Vomma and vega are two factors involved in understanding and identifying profitable option trades. The two work together in providing detail on an option's price and the option price’s sensitivity to market changes.

What news catalyst is left to cause that fear in the market is the million dollar question. Tariffs have been front run and businesses ordered a lot of stuff. Market does not care about CPI or PCE or inflation in general. JPow has waved the white flag with rate cuts. BLS will stop publishing job reports. There might be possible peace for Russia and Ukraine. 

What injects fear into the heart of this market is what I am really curious about ?

comment.body:
There is no august jobs report since new Trump appointed labor head has said publicly jobs report will not be published. Market no longer cares about PCE since Fed has lost credibility. J Pow is famous for his transitory speech in 2021 when he called Inflation transitory and maintained rates. Then again in 2022 before reacting late and hiking rates. 

Fed used to say that Inflation target was 2%. We are nowhere near 2% and real inflation is 20% as Alma said in one of her posts. Yet  now same Fed is cutting rates thanks to the pressure from Trump and claims it is because of the labor market. 

It is all one big ponzi scheme since job numbers are distorted and will not be published. Fed claims labor is cracking and is cutting. Fed is silent about their desired 2% inflation target. 

So out of the 3 catalysts you mentioned for downside, 2 will not impact much. The remaining one is NVDA earnings and they will beat easily given that capex spending for Q2 did not slow down a bit.
